title: CLI
description: Install the you-agent-factory CLI and look up the core you commands for starting a factory, submitting work, checking sessions, and reading packaged docs.
openingSummary: The you-agent-factory CLI is the command-line entrypoint for installing the factory tooling and operating long-running agent work from your machine.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers the you-agent-factory CLI: how to install the you binary, and which core commands start a factory, submit work, inspect sessions and work, or print packaged docs. It is the durable CLI lookup surface for customers who need install entrypoints and command purpose at a glance.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A factory is the long-running runtime that keeps agent work persistent. Some commands start that runtime on your machine. Other commands talk to a factory that is already running. Packaged docs commands print reference markdown without needing a live factory.

install.title: Install
install.body: Install the you CLI with the published release entrypoint for your operating system. These commands install the binary; they do not start a factory.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Install the CLI with the published macOS, Linux, or Windows install entrypoint for your operating system. Then use you to start a local factory when you need a runtime, you submit or you submit batch to enqueue work against a running factory, you session and you work to inspect live state, and you docs to read packaged reference topics offline.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web CLI reference for install entrypoints and core command purpose. It is not a full flag dump, not a sync of packaged CLI markdown, and not the harness, MCP, or configuration deep pages.
